VAL-DEPLOY-001.evidence: Vitest: `npx vitest run test/deploy/fly.test.ts --maxConcurrency=7` -> 20/20 passed. Artifact checks: `ls -la .../fly.toml` and `ls -la .../Dockerfile` both present. Content transcript confirms placeholder-safe values in fly.toml (`app = "replace-with-fly-app-name"`) and deploy-oriented Dockerfile entrypoint (`CMD ["node", "dist/relay/index.js"]`).
VAL-DEPLOY-001.reason: Deploy artifacts exist and include placeholder-first configuration consistent with safe secret handling expectations.

VAL-DEPLOY-002.evidence: Missing-tool transcript: `node dist/index.js deploy --json` with required env set returned `{"category":"flyctl","message":"flyctl CLI is not installed or not found in PATH."}` and `EXIT: 1`. Missing-auth transcript: using isolated fake flyctl (`MORS_DEPLOY_FLYCTL_PATH=<temp>/flyctl`) without `FLY_ACCESS_TOKEN` returned `{"category":"auth","message":"Fly.io deploy authentication is not configured."}` with explicit remediation (`flyctl auth login` / `FLY_ACCESS_TOKEN`) and `EXIT: 1`. Vitest deploy suite also passed these scenarios.
VAL-DEPLOY-002.reason: Both required failure paths (missing flyctl and missing deploy auth) fail closed with explicit remediation guidance.

VAL-DEPLOY-003.evidence: Dry-run transcript: `node dist/index.js deploy --dry-run --json` (isolated temp config, fake flyctl, canary env secrets) returned `status: ready` with non-sensitive config only and `EXIT: 0`. Canary redaction check on output: `rg 'CANARY_DEPLOY_SECRET_ABC123|CANARY_GH_CLIENT_987'` -> no matches (`CANARY_LEAK_CHECK: PASS`). Sensitive word scan on dry-run output (`rg -i 'password|token|secret'`) -> no matches. Shell safety suite `npx vitest run test/deploy/shell-injection-safety.test.ts --maxConcurrency=7` passed 10/10.
VAL-DEPLOY-003.reason: Deploy output remained free of canary secret values and did not disclose sensitive material in tested deploy paths.
